title: Levels of Taxonomy Used in Biology
description: Taxonomy is the practice of categorizing and naming of species. The official "scientific name" of an organism consists of its Genus and its Species Identifier in a naming system called binomial nomenclature.

title: 6 Basic Animal Classes
description: Describes the six main animal groups from simplest to most complex.

title: Pulmonary Arteries
description: Describes what the pulmonary arteries in a human body are and what they do

title: Are Whales Fish?
description: Distinctions between whales and fish are more influenced by cultural and linguistic changes than by scientific discoveries.
